summary: Russian forces reported liberating the village of Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region and striking fuel infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Poltava. Ukrainian President Zelensky said Belarusian repeater systems used for guiding Russian drones stopped operating on June 22, and mandatory evacuations were ordered in 12 settlements of Chernigov region. Russian air defenses claimed shooting down 17 guided bombs and 762 UAVs while Ukrainian forces reported destroying over 60,000 tonnes of ammunition near St. Petersburg.
key_points: Russian troops liberated Ivolzhanskoye village in Sumy region, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132)
Ukrainian President Zelensky stated that Belarusian repeater systems used to guide Russian drones ceased operation on June 22. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31144)
Mandatory evacuations were declared in 12 settlements of the Chernigov region bordering Russia and Belarus. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142)

Russian forces attacked fuel and energy facilities of Ukrnafta in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, and Naftogaz assets in Poltava, causing shutdown of fuel stations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Russian air defence reported downing 17 guided aerial bombs and 762 UAVs in a 24‑hour period. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
Russian troops destroyed Ukrainian UAV launch sites, captured 127 buildings and killed up to 90 Ukrainian soldiers in Konstantinovka. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)

Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of more than 60,000 tonnes of ammunition stored near St. Petersburg, prompting Russia to redeploy air‑defence systems to Moscow and the Kerch bridge area. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

Ukrainian drone sightings were reported over Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions, indicating ongoing UAV activity. (Source: @kpszsu/66320, @kpszsu/66324, @kpszsu/66286, @kpszsu/66281, @kpszsu/66334, @kpszsu/66331, @kpszsu/66308)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces have taken Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region and intensified strikes on Ukrainian fuel infrastructure across multiple oblasts. Zelensky reported the shutdown of Belarusian repeater systems that aided Russian drones, while Russian air defences claim high UAV kill rates. Ukraine continues evacuations in Chernigov and reports significant ammunition losses near St. Petersburg, prompting Russian air‑defence redeployments.

points: Zelensky announced that Belarusian repeater systems used for guiding Russian drones stopped operating on June 22, though their dismantlement status remains unclear. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31144)
Mandatory evacuations were ordered in 12 settlements of the Chernigov region, which borders Russia's Bryansk and Belarus's Gomel regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142)

Ukrainian officials reported that Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, as well as Naftogaz facilities in Poltava, halting fuel station operations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Ukraine claimed destruction of over 60,000 tonnes of Russian ammunition stored near St. Petersburg, influencing Russian air‑defence reallocations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

Ukrainian sources noted ongoing UAV activity over Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions, indicating continued reconnaissance and strike missions. (Source: @kpszsu/66320, @kpszsu/66324, @kpszsu/66286, @kpszsu/66281, @kpszsu/66334, @kpszsu/66331, @kpszsu/66308)
The Russian Ministry of Defence announced the liberation of Ivolzhanskoye village in Sumy region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132)
Russian forces reported striking Ukrainian fuel‑energy and transport infrastructure used by the Ukrainian Armed Forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382354)
Russian air‑defence units claimed shooting down 17 guided aerial bombs and 762 UAVs in a single day. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
Russian troops destroyed Ukrainian UAV storage and launch sites, captured 127 buildings and killed up to 90 Ukrainian soldiers in Konstantinovka. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
In the western direction, Russian forces seized 9 strongpoints and 38 buildings in Krasny Liman. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
Ukrainian evacuations in Chernigov region reflect heightened threat along the border with Russia and Belarus. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142)
Ukrainian drone activity reported over Sumy and surrounding areas suggests continued contest for airspace in the eastern sector. (Source: @kpszsu/66320, @kpszsu/66331, @kpszsu/66308)
Russian claims of destroying UAV launch sites and capturing buildings in Konstantinovka indicate offensive operations in the east. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
Ukrainian fuel depot attacks in Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk highlight Russian pressure on logistical nodes in the eastern theater. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Ukrainian drone sightings over Sumy and Kharkiv regions point to ongoing reconnaissance along the northern front. (Source: @kpszsu/66286, @kpszsu/66320)
Russian air‑defence reports of downing 762 UAVs suggest high aerial activity over the northern approaches. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
The cessation of Belarusian repeater systems may affect Russian UAV operations targeting northern Ukrainian positions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31144)
No specific ground engagements were detailed for the northern front in the provided messages.
Russian drone strikes hit Ukrnafta fuel complexes in Zaporizhzhia and Mykolaiv, disrupting southern supply lines. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Ukrainian reports of UAV activity near Zaporizhzhia city indicate continued contest for air superiority in the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66334)
No explicit Russian ground advances were reported for the southern front in the current data.
Ukrainian evacuation orders were not mentioned for southern oblasts in the supplied messages.
Russian air‑defence forces claimed shooting down 17 guided bombs and 762 UAVs within 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)

Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity over multiple oblasts, including Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @kpszsu/66320, @kpszsu/66324, @kpszsu/66286, @kpszsu/66281, @kpszsu/66334, @kpszsu/66331, @kpszsu/66308)
Russian strikes on Ukrainian fuel infrastructure employed drones, indicating a reliance on UAVs for strategic targeting. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Ukrainian claim of destroying 60,000 tonnes of ammunition near St. Petersburg suggests possible air‑strike or long‑range missile activity affecting Russian logistics. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)

key_developments: Russian forces liberated Ivolzhanskoye village in Sumy region, expanding their control in the northeast. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132)
Zelensky announced the shutdown of Belarusian repeater systems that guided Russian drones on June 22, potentially degrading Russian UAV effectiveness. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31144)
Mandatory evacuations were ordered in 12 Chernigov settlements bordering Russia and Belarus, reflecting heightened security concerns. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142)
Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, and Naftogaz assets in Poltava, halting fuel station operations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82157)
Russian air‑defence claimed downing 17 guided bombs and 762 UAVs in a single day, indicating intense aerial combat. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)

Ukrainian forces reported destroying over 60,000 tonnes of Russian ammunition near St. Petersburg, prompting Russian air‑defence redeployments to Moscow and the Kerch bridge. (Source: @UkraineNow/82148)
Russian troops destroyed Ukrainian UAV launch sites, captured 127 buildings and killed up to 90 Ukrainian soldiers in Konstantinovka. (Source: @tass_agency/382355)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian advances in the Sumy region and intensified drone strikes on fuel infrastructure increase pressure on Ukrainian logistics and rear areas. The cessation of Belarusian repeater systems may reduce Russian UAV guidance capability, but Russian air‑defence claims suggest they are still contesting the skies. Ukrainian evacuations in Chernigov highlight vulnerability of border settlements, while large‑scale ammunition losses near St. Petersburg indicate Ukraine's growing strike reach, potentially prompting further Russian air‑defence reallocations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing border evacuations and monitoring UAV activity across multiple fronts, while publicly highlighting Belarusian system shutdowns to signal reduced Russian support. Russia continues to claim air‑defence successes and ground gains, reallocating air‑defence assets to protect strategic locations like Moscow and the Kerch bridge. Both sides are leveraging information operations to shape domestic and international perceptions of the conflict.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future monitoring should focus on the operational status of Belarusian repeater equipment and any Russian attempts to replace or bypass them. UAV flight patterns over Sumy, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia warrant close analysis for targeting trends. The reported loss of 60,000 tonnes of ammunition near St. Petersburg suggests a possible new strike capability that could affect Russian rear‑area security.
